title: Missing record classes and coverage gaps

items.title: No interface records for UDF API surface
items.observation: The schema defines item_type=interface, but architecture_items.jsonl contains zero interface records. UDF/procedure surfaces (legal.active_at, legal.resolve_citation, legal.verify_evidence, legal.find_requirements) are not modeled as interfaces.
items.verification: Add interface records per documented UDF/procedure with input/output type fields captured in summary or governed_artifacts.
non_claims: Does not specify UDF implementation.
Does not require runtime contracts beyond record fields.

items.title: No data_entity records for core legal graph types
items.observation: Schema supports item_type=data_entity. LegalAct, ActEdition, EvidenceSpan, LegalUnit, NormStatement, TextChunk, SourceDocument, SourceBlock are referenced extensively in source PRDs but absent as registry records. The layer legal-evidence has zero nodes.
items.verification: Add data_entity records anchored to the corresponding PRD sections; link them via evidenced_by and depends_on to existing parser/temporal items.
non_claims: Does not assert specific schema property names.
Does not require implementation of these entities.

items.title: No component records for runtime services
items.observation: Schema supports item_type=component. Legal Nexus orchestrator, ETL pipeline, FalkorDB store, Embedding service, KnowQL planner, Evidence verifier are referenced as services but not modeled as component records. The layer api-product has zero nodes.
items.verification: Add component records for each named service with governed_artifacts pointing at scripts/* or future module paths; link via implements edges to the corresponding requirements.
non_claims: Does not commit to building these services in M004.
Does not require specific deployment topology.

items.title: No quality_scenario records for NFRs
items.observation: Schema supports item_type=quality_scenario. NFRs such as determinism, auditability, idempotency, performance target, legal precision, explainability, scalability, privacy/local-first compatibility are referenced in PRD but not represented as registry records. The layer observability-operability has zero nodes.
items.verification: Add quality_scenario records for each NFR with measurable verification text and proof_gate links where bounded.
non_claims: Does not set specific SLOs.
Does not require runtime measurement in M004.

items.title: No viewpoint records for stakeholder perspectives
items.observation: Schema supports item_type=viewpoint. PRD describes target users (Legal Data Engineer, AI/RAG Engineer, Legal Expert, AI Agent) but no viewpoint records exist. uses_viewpoint edge type is unused.
items.verification: Add viewpoint records for each stakeholder role; use uses_viewpoint edges to attach the perspective to relevant requirements or quality scenarios.
non_claims: Does not assert that viewpoint records are required for M004 acceptance.
Does not propose a specific viewpoint catalog.

items.title: FR-by-FR coverage of source PRDs is absent
items.observation: The registry contains no per-FR records anchored to prd/01_general_idea.md, prd/02_architecture.md, prd/03_PRD.md. Coverage of FR-1..FR-31 and NFR-1..NFR-9 cannot be measured from the current registry alone.
items.verification: Add at least requirement records for each FR and quality_scenario records for each NFR with source_anchors pointing at the corresponding PRD section.
non_claims: Does not require all FRs to be implemented.
Does not assert that each FR must be a separate record.

items.title: No coverage of derived PRD planning documents
items.observation: Sibling planning documents (prd/05_final_architecture_review.md, prd/06_m002_cypher_safety_contract.md, prd/07_m002_text_to_cypher_recommendation.md, prd/08_m003_minimax_pyo3_functioning_proof.md, prd/09_architecture_planning_verification_research.md, prd/10_m006_parser_graph_staging_recommendation.md) are referenced from at most a couple of source_anchors. There is no systematic evidence record for each.
items.verification: For each sibling planning doc, decide whether to add an evidence record or document why it is intentionally not registered.
non_claims: Does not require duplicating doc content into the registry.
Does not assert all sibling docs are equally important.

items.title: No supersession edges, despite explicit schema support
items.observation: Schema defines supersedes and superseded_by edge types and a required superseded_by field for items with status=superseded. The current registry contains no superseded records and no supersession edges.
items.verification: Either confirm that no records have been superseded yet (acceptable in early milestone state), or document a worked example of supersession to validate the schema branch and verifier rule end-to-end.
non_claims: Does not assert that supersession is required now.
Does not propose specific records to supersede.

items.title: No contradicts edges, despite contradiction handling in verifier
items.observation: S04 verifier policy treats active/hypothesis/bounded-evidence contradicts edges as hard failures. The current edges file contains zero contradiction edges; this branch of verifier behavior is untested by the registry itself.
items.verification: Either confirm absence is by design, or add a fixture-only contradiction edge in tests/ to validate the verifier branch.
non_claims: Does not require introducing contradictions in the production registry.
Does not assert that absence indicates incorrect behavior.

items.title: No CI hook or regenerate-on-change automation documented
items.observation: README documents the manual workflow (extract → build → verify → views) but not whether a CI hook enforces it on every commit that touches PRD/GSD/architecture/scripts.
items.verification: Add a CI-integration section to README describing the recommended hook (pre-commit, GitHub Action, or both) and the exact commands.
non_claims: Does not require a specific CI provider.
Does not commit to a particular hook scope.

items.title: No coverage metric ratio (FR-records / FR-source-count)
items.observation: Derived views report node counts per layer but no measure of how completely PRD FRs/NFRs are projected into the registry. There is no 'covered / total' ratio for source PRD requirements.
items.verification: Extend the views generator with a coverage section that counts FR/NFR anchors per source file versus the total references in the file, with an explicit non-claim that 100% projection is not a product readiness signal.
non_claims: Does not assert that 100% coverage is the right goal.
Does not assert that registry coverage equals product completeness.

items.title: No risks edges connecting risk records to dependent items
items.observation: Schema defines edge type risks. RISK-OVERCLAIM-RUNTIME has one inbound checked_by edge but no outbound risks edges to the records it threatens (parser, retrieval, generated-Cypher records).
items.verification: Add risks edges from RISK-OVERCLAIM-RUNTIME to the records that would be affected, so graph queries can answer 'what does this risk threaten?'.
non_claims: Does not require linking the risk to every record.
Does not propose a specific risk surface inventory.

items.title: No security-safety records beyond redaction and LLM authority
items.observation: security-safety layer has 3 nodes: REQ-R022 (redaction), REQ-R028 (LLM non-authoritative), RISK-OVERCLAIM-RUNTIME. There are no records for access control, authentication, audit log integrity, secret handling beyond redaction, or supply-chain safety for embedding models / FalkorDB binaries.
items.verification: Add at least proof_gate or requirement records for access control, audit log integrity, and embedding-model supply chain, anchored to PRD or GSD evidence.
non_claims: Does not assert current product is insecure.
Does not commit to specific security mechanisms.

items.title: No temporal-model records beyond a single proof gate
items.observation: temporal-model layer contains only GATE-G005 (same-date multi-edition conflict). PRD defines edition_date, valid_from, valid_to, effective_from, effective_to, status, temporal_confidence as central temporal semantics, none represented as data_entity or requirement records.
items.verification: Add data_entity record for temporal property bundle and requirement records for temporal status semantics, anchored to PRD sections.
non_claims: Does not specify implementation of temporal storage.
Does not require a complete temporal model in M004.

items.title: No assumptions modeled beyond one orphan
items.observation: Only one assumption record exists (ASSUMP-PRD-SOURCE-TRUTH, orphan per R04-INC-003). PRD and architecture documents rely on multiple unstated assumptions (e.g., ODT format stability from Гарант, FalkorDBLite parity with Docker FalkorDB, embedding model availability, Russian-language tokenization quality).
items.verification: Add assumption records for the most load-bearing implicit assumptions and link them via has_assumption from the records that depend on them.
non_claims: Does not assert each assumption needs an explicit record.
Does not enumerate every implicit assumption.
Gaps describe what the registry does not project, not what the product lacks.
Filling a gap with a record does not validate the underlying claim.
Does not promote any record into architecture_items.jsonl.
